title: RequestSpecification (Reusable Setup)
overview: Avoid repetition by creating reusable Request and Response Specifications.
whyItMatters: In a large test suite, repeating the base URI, authentication headers, and content types in every test makes the code hard to maintain. Specifications centralize this configuration.
explanation: A `RequestSpecification` is built using the `RequestSpecBuilder`. You add common properties (like base URI, auth, headers) to the builder and build the specification. You then pass this specification to the `given().spec(...)` method. Similarly, a `ResponseSpecification` (built with `ResponseSpecBuilder`) centralizes common assertions (like asserting 200 OK or content type).
keyPoints: Apply specs using `.spec()` in the `given()` and `then()` blocks.
Reduces code duplication and enforces consistency across tests.
codeExample:
// 1. Define the specification (usually in a BaseTest class)
RequestSpecification reqSpec = new RequestSpecBuilder()
    .setBaseUri("https://api.example.com")
    .addHeader("Authorization", "Bearer token123")
    .setContentType(ContentType.JSON)
    .build();

// 2. Use it in a test
given().
    spec(reqSpec).
    body("{ \"name\": \"Test\" }").
when().
    post("/items").
then().
    statusCode(201);
realWorldExample: You have 50 API tests that all target the staging environment and require a JWT token. Instead of writing `baseUri(...)` and `header(...)` 50 times, you define a `RequestSpecification` in a `@BeforeClass` and apply it to every request.
commonMistakes: Mutating a shared `RequestSpecification` instance within a specific test, which accidentally affects other tests running in parallel.
Over-complicating specs by adding endpoint-specific logic to global specifications.
bestPractices: Define base specifications in a `@BeforeSuite` or a Base API Test class.
Use `RestAssured.requestSpecification` for global defaults if you don't run tests in parallel, but prefer explicit `.spec(reqSpec)` for thread-safe parallel execution.
Combine with `ResponseSpecification` for common assertions like `statusCode(200)` and performance `time(lessThan(...))`.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you avoid duplicating base URIs and authentication headers across multiple API tests?
interviewQuestions.a: By using a `RequestSpecification`. You build it once with `RequestSpecBuilder` and apply it to the `given()` block of each test, or assign it globally to `RestAssured.requestSpecification`.

interviewQuestions.q: Can you use both RequestSpecification and ResponseSpecification in the same test?
interviewQuestions.a: Yes, `given().spec(requestSpec)` applies the request setup, and `then().spec(responseSpec)` applies the common assertions.

revisionSummary: Centralize request configurations (auth, headers, base URI) using RequestSpecBuilder.
